QUAD // ABYSS CLIMB

A one-tile-wide climb against a bottomless abyss.

QUAD // ABYSS CLIMB is a free browser climbing game. A single connected trail winds upward forever — one tile wide, never twice as wide — and a hungry abyss rises from below, getting faster every ten seconds. Your only job is to stay on the ribbon and keep climbing before the darkness catches up. There's nothing to install and no account to make; it works the same on a phone in your pocket or a laptop at your desk.

HOW TO PLAY

  • Up moves forward one tile along the trail.
  • Left / Right shifts you sideways one tile, following the path's shape.
  • To move diagonally, press two directions in sequence (e.g. Left, then Up).
  • Step off the visible trail — even by one tile — and it's game over instantly.
  • Moving quickly in a row builds a boost streak, which doubles the points from every step while it lasts.

SCORING & DIFFICULTY

Every step earns points — 10 normally, 20 while boosting. The abyss below starts slow but compresses its interval by roughly 28% every ten seconds, so runs get noticeably tighter the longer you survive. The cave itself changes with your score too: every 1000 points the background shifts to a new color theme, with more twinkling detail added at higher tiers — the deeper (and higher) you climb, the more the world lights up.

ONLINE LEADERBOARD

Pick a nickname and your best score is saved to a public, worldwide leaderboard — no password, no account. You can switch nicknames at any time from the ready screen or the game-over screen if you want to start a fresh run under a different name.

ABOUT THIS SITE

QUAD // ABYSS CLIMB is an independently built, ad-supported browser game. It's free to play and works on any modern phone or desktop browser. See our Privacy Policy for details on the minimal data (nickname + score) the leaderboard stores.
